Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Transportation Act (air passenger protection)
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to amend the Canada Transportation Act (air passenger protection)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Transportation Act to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: require the Canadian Transportation Agency to make certain regulations in relation to an air carrierâs obligations towards its passengers, including regulations requiring an air carrier to pay minimum monetary compensation for inconvenience in the case of a flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding that is not caused by extraordinary circumstances;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: set out what constitutes âextraordinary circumstancesâ for the purposes of the requirement to pay minimum monetary compensation;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: increase the maximum amounts of the fines payable in respect of certain offences under the Act that are punishable on summary conviction; and

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: increase the maximum amounts of the administrative monetary penalties that the Canadian Transportation Agency may, by regulation, prescribe for the contravention of requirements under the Act that may be proceeded with as a violation.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Strengthening Air Passenger Protection Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 55(1) of the Canada Transportation Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: denial of boardingâmeans the refusal to carry a passenger on a flight on which they hold a confirmed reservation â or on which they held a confirmed reservation before their itinerary was amended by the carrier without their consent â for a reason other than a failure by the passenger to

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: present themselves at the airport at the required time for check-in; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: comply with health, safety or security requirements;â(refus dâembarquement)

Definition.Text: flight cancellationâmeans the failure to operate a flight that was scheduled and on which at least one passenger held a confirmed reservation;â(annulation de vol)

Subsection.Text: Section 55 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

For the purposes of this Part, a passenger holds a confirmed reservation on a flight if the passenger has been issued a ticket or other document that indicates that the carrier has accepted and registered the reservation.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 85.1:
For greater certainty, the burden is on the carrier to establish, on a balance of probabilities, the cause of a flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding.

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 86.11(1)(b)(i) to (iv) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the carrierâs obligation to pay minimum monetary compensation for inconvenience when the flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding was not caused by extraordinary circumstances, and the amount of that compensation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the carrierâs obligation to automatically pay the minimum monetary compensation referred to in subparagraph (i) to each passenger affected by the flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding as soon as feasible after its occurrence, without a request for compensation necessarily having been made by the passenger,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the minimum standards of treatment of passengers that the carrier is required to meet, regardless of the cause of the flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the carrierâs obligation to provide timely information and assistance to passengers, regardless of the cause of the flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding, and

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the carrierâs obligation to provide a passenger who chooses not to travel with a refund â using the method used for the original payment â for the cost of all unused services purchased in connection with the flight, regardless of the cause of the flight delay, flight cancellation or denial of boarding;

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 86.11(1)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing the minimum compensation for lost, delayed or damaged baggage that the carrier is required to pay;

Subsection.Text: Section 86.11 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

For the purposes of subparagraph (1)(b)(i), extraordinary circumstances means circumstances that were not inherent to normal operations, were beyond the carrierâs control, could not have been avoided even if the carrier had taken all reasonable measures, and were not attributable, in whole or in part, directly or indirectly, to any of the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: acts or omissions of the carrier, its agents or servants, or any third party with whom the carrier has a contractual relationship;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any situation that was known or should have been known to the carrier at the time the ticket was sold to the passenger; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: aircraft maintenance or safety issues, other than those caused by acts of sabotage or terrorism or a manufacturing defect in an aircraft identified by the aircraftâs manufacturer or a competent authority.

Section.Text: Paragraphs 174(a) and (b) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an individual, to a fine not exceeding $50,000; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a corporation, to a fine not exceeding $250,000.

Section.Text: Section 176 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Time limit for commencement of proceedings
Section.Text: Proceedings by way of summary conviction in respect of an offence under this Act may be instituted not later than three years after the day on which the subject-matter of the proceedings arose.
Paragraph 177(1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: prescribe the minimum and maximum amount payable for each violation, but the maximum amount shall not exceed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: $50,000, in the case of an individual, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: $250,000, in the case of a corporation.

Section.Text: Section 181 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Time limit for commencement of proceedings
Section.Text: Proceedings in respect of a violation may be instituted not later than three years after the day on which the subject-matter of the proceedings arose.
